w:r.w:t: Step 1: Prepare Your Code — Make These Changes First
and add these lines at the very bottom (after all your routes):
and make the scripts look like this:
Step 2: Create a GitHub Account & Upload Your Code
Now on your computer, open a terminal/command prompt
` with your actual GitHub username. It will ask
your GitHub password — use a **Personal Access Token** instead
Developer Settings → Personal Access Tokens → Generate New Token → check
→ copy it and use it as your password.
️ Step 4: Create a MySQL Database on Railway
. On the Railway dashboard, click **New Project**
. Railway will start trying to deploy — **ignore it
now**, first we need the database
. Railway creates a MySQL database. Click on it,
` — **keep this tab open**, you'll need these values
. Add each variable one by one by clicking **+ New Variable**:
Type any long random string, e.g. `
️ Step 6: Set the Build & Start Commands
. Still on your app service, go to **Settings** tab
. Find **Build Command** and set it to:
. Find **Start Command** and set it to:
You'll see a terminal/query editor — copy and paste the entire contents of your
file into it and run it
This creates all your tables and the default admin account
Alternatively, Railway has a "Variables" tab with a
— you can use a MySQL client like
to connect remotely and run the SQL file.
Go back to your app service in Railway
— Railway gives you a free URL like
Wait 2-3 minutes for the deployment to finish (watch the
Click your URL — your app should be live!
In Railway, click your app service →
tab → click the latest deployment → you'll see all the logs and any error messages.
→ Double-check your DB_ environment variables match exactly
→ Check the build logs, usually a missing dependency
→ Make sure you added the static file serving code to
after this If I made changes in the project it will directly change on the railway or no?
— but only after you push your changes to GitHub.
You edit code → git push → Railway detects it → Auto rebuilds & deploys
Every time you make a change, just run these 3 commands:
Your live site is updated in ~2-3 minutes
In Railway → click your app service →
tab → you'll see a new deployment appear with a loading spinner → turns green when done
One thing to be careful about
(adding a new table, adding a column, etc.), Railway will
automatically update your database. You'd have to manually run the SQL changes in the Railway MySQL query editor.
if you add a new column to a table:
You'd paste that directly into the Railway database query editor.
If you use a custom domain (like pullzone.com)
If you buy your own domain and connect it to Railway:
Railway shows you a DNS record to add
Go to wherever you bought your domain (GoDaddy, Namecheap, etc.) → add that DNS record
automatically issues a free SSL certificate
So your custom domain also becomes HTTPS automatically — Railway handles everything.
You never have to manually configure SSL/HTTPS with Railway. It's always handled for you.
